name: DMR ESQL Patterns
description: Canonical ES|QL query patterns and field paths for writing queries against the dmr-raw-000002 index or the dmr-cars-flat view. Activate when writing ES|QL for a DMR question not covered by a dedicated tool — for field path lookup, query template selection, syntax rules, or data quality guard application.
content:
Load this skill when you need to write ES|QL against the DMR dataset. Follow this workflow:
1. Check field-shortcuts for the correct field path or use the flat view columns (brand, model, fuel, etc.)
2. Select the nearest pattern from query-patterns and adapt it
3. Apply the rules in syntax-rules (double quotes, LIMIT, data quality guards)
4. Execute with platform.core.execute_esql
5. On error: read the message, check the field path, retry once
6. If it fails twice: state the question cannot be answered with the available data

referenced_content.name: syntax-rules
referenced_content.content:
CRITICAL RULES — violating these causes errors:

1. String literals: ALWAYS double quotes. 'El' fails. "El" works.
2. LIMIT is required. Use LIMIT 20 for ranked lists, LIMIT 1 for single aggregates, LIMIT 50 for time series, LIMIT 100 for record lists.
3. Keyword fields: most string fields need .keyword for filtering/grouping (e.g. KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword). Helper fields _drivkraft_primaer and _foerste_registrering_aar do NOT need .keyword.
4. Null check: IS NOT NULL (not != null)
5. Range check: field BETWEEN a AND b (inclusive)
6. Date part extraction: DATE_EXTRACT("year", field) not YEAR(field)
7. String-to-number: TO_INTEGER(string_field)
8. Rounding: ROUND(AVG(field), 1) — always wrap aggregates in ROUND for readability
9. For analytics averages over numeric fields with outliers: use CLAMP(field, min, max)
10. For individual ranked lists: use CASE to return NULL for out-of-range values (NOT CLAMP — CLAMP makes corrupt values look valid in lists)
11. INLINE STATS runs after STATS and computes over the grouped rows — use for fleet vs group comparisons
12. LIMIT BY: LIMIT n BY field — top-N per group (must be followed by a global LIMIT)
13. Per-aggregation WHERE: COUNT(*) WHERE field == "value" — conditional count within a single STATS
14. Never write mutations. Only FROM/WHERE/EVAL/STATS/SORT/LIMIT/KEEP/CASE queries.
15. Default base for passenger cars: FROM dmr-raw-000002 | WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"

DATA QUALITY GUARDS (always apply to these fields):
- power_kw: CLAMP(field, 1, 750) for stats; CASE(field >= 1 AND field <= 750, field, NULL) for lists
- speed_kmh: CASE(field >= 50 AND field <= 400, field, NULL)
- kerb_kg: CASE(field >= 300 AND field <= 5000, field, NULL)
- towing_kg: CASE(field >= 1 AND field <= 5000, field, NULL)
- doors: WHERE field BETWEEN 2 AND 6
- seats: WHERE field BETWEEN 1 AND 9
- year: AND _foerste_registrering_aar >= 1900 AND _foerste_registrering_aar <= DATE_EXTRACT("year", NOW())
16. IN clause uses PARENTHESES not square brackets: field IN ("a", "b") — NOT field IN ["a", "b"]
17. Top-N individual records: use SORT + LIMIT + KEEP, not STATS MAX(). STATS MAX(field) on a column with many NULLs returns NULL for all groups.
    CORRECT: FROM dmr-cars-flat | WHERE speed_kmh IS NOT NULL | SORT speed_kmh DESC | KEEP brand, model, speed_kmh | LIMIT 10
    WRONG:   FROM dmr-cars-flat | STATS max_speed = MAX(speed_kmh) BY brand, model | SORT max_speed DESC | LIMIT 10
18. Always add WHERE field IS NOT NULL before SORT on numeric columns — otherwise NULL values dominate results.
19. GROUP BY does not exist in ES|QL. Use STATS ... BY instead.
20. If you are stuck running the same query more than twice: stop, load a different pattern, and try a completely different approach.
21. `_source` is NOT a valid ES|QL field. Use KEEP to select specific columns instead.
22. Danish special characters (æ, ø, å) in field names: always use the ASCII-safe field paths from field-shortcuts. Never type Danish characters directly in ES|QL field names.
23. The flat view (dmr-cars-flat) is pre-filtered to registered Personbil. Never add WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus or KoeretoejArtNavn or WHERE Registreret to the flat view — these are not columns.
24. Year range guard: when filtering or grouping by year or model_year, always add WHERE year >= 1990 AND year <= DATE_EXTRACT("year", NOW()) to exclude corrupt future years (the data contains entries with model_year 2104, 2122, etc.).
25. Brand case in the flat view: NOT all brands are uppercase. "Polestar", "Aiways", "Xpeng", "Cupra" are stored with mixed case. If brand query returns 0 results, run the raw index discovery query to find the exact stored case, then return to dmr-cars-flat for the actual data query.
26. After raw index brand discovery, ALWAYS switch back to dmr-cars-flat for model/stats queries. The raw index has no `model` alias — use only the flat view for brand+model breakdowns.
27. TO_UPPER() and TO_LOWER() are the correct ES|QL case functions. UPPER() and LOWER() do NOT exist and will error. Use TO_UPPER(brand) == "POLESTAR" for case-insensitive brand matching — this handles all mixed-case brands (Polestar, Cupra, Xpeng, etc.) without a discovery query.
28. taxi_suitable flag (170,246 cars) = type-approved for taxi, NOT currently a taxi. For actual taxis: WHERE usage == "Taxikørsel" (5,090 cars).
29. km_per_liter for EVs is an energy-equivalent (~67 km/L) not real fuel use. For meaningful fuel efficiency: WHERE fuel != "El" AND km_per_liter > 1 AND km_per_liter <= 30. Use km_per_liter_ev column for EV energy efficiency comparisons.
30. awd column: valid values are 1 (FWD/RWD) and 2 (AWD). Values 0, 3, 4, 12, 14 are corrupt and now filtered to NULL in the flat view. Always use awd == 2 for AWD, never awd >= 2.
31. body_style is NULL for 14% of cars. For clean body-style queries add WHERE body_style IS NOT NULL.
32. Minimum count filter for comparative brand queries: without it, brands with 1-3 vehicles dominate rankings with unrepresentative values (e.g. a moped brand wins fuel efficiency). Always add WHERE count >= N after the STATS grouping: use count >= 500 for fuel/emissions, count >= 1000 for power/speed, count >= 100 for inspection rates.
33. TO_UPPER() + LIKE for partial brand names: users often type "mercedes" when the registry says "MERCEDES-BENZ", or "alfa" for "ALFA ROMEO". Use WHERE TO_UPPER(brand) LIKE "MERCEDES%" to match all brands starting with that prefix. LIKE on keyword fields is case-sensitive, so combine with TO_UPPER().
34. Model name matching: use TO_UPPER(model) == "MODEL Y" or model IN ("Model Y", "Y") to handle case and spelling variants. Never rely on exact case for model names.
35. TO_UPPER() + LIKE does NOT work — it returns 0 results. LIKE requires a keyword field, not a transformed value. Correct patterns:
    - Exact: TO_UPPER(brand) == "POLESTAR" (works)
    - Prefix: brand LIKE "LAND%" (works, for uppercase brands)
    - Regex: brand RLIKE "(?i).*rover.*" (works, case-insensitive partial match)
    NEVER write: TO_UPPER(brand) LIKE "%pattern%" — it returns 0 rows.
36. DATE_EXTRACT on the flat view year/model_year columns: these are already integers. DATE_EXTRACT("year", year) will fail with "must be datetime". Just use WHERE year >= 2020 directly.
37. Visualization rules: call create_visualization at most once per user question. Normal chart creation should call create_visualization with query=<ES|QL string> and chartType="xy" or "pie"; always include LIMIT in chart queries. For fallback visualization tags after create_visualization is unavailable/fails, or for category distributions with more than 8 categories, use the ID from the ES|QL {"type": "esql_results"} result, never the {"type": "query"} result.
38. ALFA ROMEO is stored as "ALFA" in the DMR registry. Use brand == "ALFA" or brand RLIKE "(?i)alfa.*" to find Alfa Romeo vehicles.
39. Date strings MUST use double quotes: "2023-01-01" not '2023-01-01'. Single-quoted dates cause parse errors.
40. Date extraction uses DATE_EXTRACT not DATE_FORMAT:
    DATE_EXTRACT("month_of_year", date_field) → month as integer (1-12)
    DATE_EXTRACT("year", date_field) → year as integer
    DATE_EXTRACT("day_of_month", date_field) → day as integer (1-31)
41. Date range filtering on a date column:
    WHERE first_reg_date >= "2023-01-01" AND first_reg_date < "2024-01-01"
    Do NOT use DATE_FORMAT for grouping — use DATE_EXTRACT.
42. For monthly registration seasonality questions, redirect to dmr-history agent (dmr-car-registration-by-month dedicated tool).
    Monthly breakdown for a specific year: use DATE_EXTRACT("month_of_year", first_reg_date) BY month in dmr-cars-flat.
43. EVAL must be a separate pipeline step — never inside STATS BY. 
    WRONG: FROM x | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY EVAL reg_year = DATE_EXTRACT("year", field)
    CORRECT: FROM x | EVAL reg_year = DATE_EXTRACT("year", field) | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY reg_year
44. For year-based grouping with date filtering: use first_reg_date for range filter + the year integer column for grouping:
    FROM dmr-cars-flat | WHERE first_reg_date >= "2005-01-01" AND first_reg_date < "2025-01-01" | STATS count = COUNT(*) BY year | SORT year ASC
    Do NOT try to extract year from first_reg_date — the year column already exists.
45. YEAR() function does not exist. To extract year from a date field use DATE_EXTRACT("year", date_field) in a separate EVAL step.

referenced_content.name: field-shortcuts
referenced_content.content:
CRITICAL RULES FOR STRING FIELDS:
1. BRAND NAMES ARE UPPERCASE in the registry: TESLA, VOLKSWAGEN, POLESTAR, BMW, TOYOTA, FORD, AUDI, MERCEDES-BENZ
   Always use .keyword field with exact uppercase: KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn.keyword == "TESLA"
   If you get 0 results: run a discovery query to find the exact stored value.
   Discovery: FROM dmr-raw-000002 | STATS c = COUNT(*) BY KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejBetegnelseStruktur.KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn.keyword | SORT c DESC | LIMIT 100

2. LIKE ONLY WORKS ON .keyword FIELDS (case-sensitive). LIKE on text fields always returns 0.
   WORKS: KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn.keyword LIKE "%POLESTAR%"
   BROKEN: KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn LIKE "%polestar%"

3. FOR FILTERING AND GROUPING always use .keyword variant of string fields.
   WORKS: WHERE KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn.keyword == "TESLA" | STATS BY KoeretoejModelTypeNavn.keyword
   UNRELIABLE: WHERE KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn == "Tesla" (text field, case behavior inconsistent)

4. FLAT VIEW NOT AVAILABLE FALLBACK: If dmr-cars-flat returns index_not_found, use FROM dmr-raw-000002 immediately.

FLAT VIEW COLUMNS (when dmr-cars-flat exists):
FROM dmr-cars-flat  — registered Personbil only
FROM dmr-vehicles-flat  — all registered vehicles
Columns: brand, model, fuel, color, year, power_kw, kerb_kg, body_style, cylinders, doors, seats, co2_gkm, awd, is_leased, is_veteran, is_accident_damaged, inspection_result, towing_kg, speed_kmh, plate, usage
Data quality already applied — no extra guards needed.
Brand/fuel/color columns: case-sensitive strings. Fuel: "El", "Benzin", "Diesel", "El/benzin", "Brint". Colors: "Grå", "Sort", "Hvid", "Blå", "Rød".

RAW INDEX (dmr-raw-000002) FIELD PATHS:
brand (keyword):   KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejBetegnelseStruktur.KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn.keyword
model (keyword):   KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejBetegnelseStruktur.Model.KoeretoejModelTypeNavn.keyword
fuel (no keyword): _drivkraft_primaer  — Values: "El", "Benzin", "Diesel", "El/benzin", "Brint", "Gas"
year (integer):    _foerste_registrering_aar
color (keyword):   KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejFarveStruktur.FarveTypeStruktur.FarveTypeNavn.keyword
body_style:        KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KarrosseriTypeStruktur.KarrosseriTypeNavn.keyword
power_kw:          KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejMotorStruktur.KoeretoejMotorStoersteEffekt
kerb_kg:           KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejOplysningKoereklarVaegtMinimum
towing_kg:         KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejOplysningTilkoblingsvaegtMedBremser
speed_kmh:         KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejOplysningMaksimumHastighed
cylinders:         KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejMotorStruktur.KoeretoejMotorCylinderAntal
doors:             KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejOplysningAntalDoere
seats:             KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejOplysningSiddepladserMinimum
co2_gkm:           _co2_g_per_km_primaer
awd:               KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejOplysningTraekkendeAkslerListe  (1=FWD/RWD, 2=AWD)
is_leased:         LeasingGyldigFra IS NOT NULL  (boolean expression, not a column)
is_leased (date):  LeasingGyldigFra IS NOT NULL AND LeasingGyldigTil >= NOW()
is_veteran:        KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejOplysningVeteranKoeretoejOriginal == TRUE
is_accident:       KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejOplysningTrafikskade == TRUE
inspection:        SynResultatStruktur.SynResultatSynsResultat.keyword  — "Godkendt", "KanGodkendesVedOmsynAfOmsynsvirksomhed", "IkkeGodkendt"
permit:            TilladelseSamling.Tilladelse.TilladelseStruktur.TilladelseTypeStruktur.TilladelseTypeNavn.keyword
usage:             KoeretoejAnvendelseStruktur.KoeretoejAnvendelseNavn.keyword
plate:             RegistreringNummerNummer

referenced_content.name: query-patterns
referenced_content.content:
PATTERN 0 — Brand/model discovery (use FIRST when brand name is unknown or query returns 0)
Run this before filtering to find the exact stored brand name (brands are UPPERCASE):
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| STATS count = COUNT(*) BY brand = KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejBetegnelseStruktur.KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn.keyword
| WHERE count >= 10 AND brand IS NOT NULL
| SORT count DESC | LIMIT 100
Then use the exact value found: KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn.keyword == "EXACT_BRAND_FROM_LIST"

BRAND+MODEL lookup (use after finding exact brand name):
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
  AND KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejBetegnelseStruktur.KoeretoejMaerkeTypeNavn.keyword == "{BRAND_UPPERCASE}"
| STATS count = COUNT(*) BY model = KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejBetegnelseStruktur.Model.KoeretoejModelTypeNavn.keyword
| SORT count DESC | LIMIT 20

PATTERN 1 — Count by category field
Use when: 'How many cars by [fuel/colour/body style]?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
| STATS count = COUNT(*) BY {category_field}
| SORT count DESC | LIMIT 20

PATTERN 2 — Numeric stats by group
Use when: 'What is the average [power/weight/CO2] by [fuel/brand/body style]?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
| EVAL value_clean = CLAMP({numeric_field}, {min}, {max})
| STATS count = COUNT(*), avg_val = ROUND(AVG(value_clean), 1), p50_val = PERCENTILE(value_clean, 50)
  BY group = {category_field}
| WHERE count >= 100
| SORT avg_val DESC | LIMIT 20

PATTERN 3 — Filter then group
Use when: 'Top brands among electric/diesel/red/veteran cars?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
  AND {filter_field} == "{filter_value}"
| STATS count = COUNT(*) BY {group_field}
| SORT count DESC | LIMIT 20

PATTERN 4 — Time series by registration year
Use when: 'How has X changed year by year?' (omit Registreret filter for all-time)
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
  AND _foerste_registrering_aar >= {start_year}
  AND _foerste_registrering_aar <= DATE_EXTRACT("year", NOW())
  AND {optional_filter}
| STATS count = COUNT(*) BY year = _foerste_registrering_aar
| SORT year ASC | LIMIT 50

PATTERN 5 — Compare two groups with per-aggregation WHERE
Use when: 'How does [metric] compare between El and Benzin?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
| EVAL clean = CLAMP({numeric_field}, {min}, {max})
| STATS
    a_count = COUNT(*) WHERE {group_field} == "{value_a}",
    a_avg   = ROUND(AVG(clean) WHERE {group_field} == "{value_a}", 1),
    b_count = COUNT(*) WHERE {group_field} == "{value_b}",
    b_avg   = ROUND(AVG(clean) WHERE {group_field} == "{value_b}", 1)
| LIMIT 1

PATTERN 6 — Above/below fleet average (INLINE STATS)
Use when: 'Which brands have above-average [power/CO2/weight]?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
| EVAL clean = CLAMP({numeric_field}, {min}, {max})
| STATS group_avg = AVG(clean) BY group = {category_field}
| INLINE STATS fleet_avg = AVG(group_avg)
| WHERE group_avg > fleet_avg
| SORT group_avg DESC | LIMIT 20

PATTERN 7 — Top-N per group (LIMIT BY)
Use when: 'Top 3 models per brand?', 'Most common colour per body style?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
| STATS count = COUNT(*) BY parent = {parent_field}, child = {child_field}
| SORT count DESC
| LIMIT {n} BY parent
| LIMIT 100

PATTERN 8 — Boolean/flag count with percentage
Use when: 'How many cars have [flag]? What share?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
| STATS total = COUNT(*), matching = COUNT(*) WHERE {flag_condition},
    pct = ROUND(COUNT(*) WHERE {flag_condition} * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1)
| LIMIT 1

PATTERN 9 — Multi-condition filter count
Use when: 'How many [red + electric + AWD] cars?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
  AND {field_1} == "{value_1}"
  AND {field_2} == "{value_2}"
  AND {numeric_field} >= {threshold}
| STATS count = COUNT(*) | LIMIT 1

PATTERN 10 — Decade bucketing
Use when: 'How has X changed by decade?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
  AND _foerste_registrering_aar >= 1950 AND _foerste_registrering_aar <= DATE_EXTRACT("year", NOW())
| EVAL decade = CASE(
    _foerste_registrering_aar < 1960, "1950s", _foerste_registrering_aar < 1970, "1960s",
    _foerste_registrering_aar < 1980, "1970s", _foerste_registrering_aar < 1990, "1980s",
    _foerste_registrering_aar < 2000, "1990s", _foerste_registrering_aar < 2010, "2000s",
    _foerste_registrering_aar < 2020, "2010s", "2020s")
| STATS count = COUNT(*), {optional_metric_stat} BY decade, {optional_group}
| SORT decade ASC | LIMIT 40

PATTERN 10b — Write-off / Totalskadet / Mangel / Skadet / lifecycle (USE THIS for any write-off question)
Use when: 'How many cars are totalskadet / write-offs / written-off / Mangel / Skadet?', 'Which brands have the most totalskadede cars?', 'Have totalskadede cars increased over time?'
CRITICAL: ~99.99 % of write-offs are on Afmeldt records. NEVER add `KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret"` or query `dmr-cars-flat` — both hide the answer.
Use the raw index without status filter, OR the dmr-cars-all-flat view:
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
  AND KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejBlokeringAarsagListeStruktur.KoeretoejBlokeringAarsagListe.KoeretoejBlokeringAarsag.KoeretoejBlokeringAarsagTypeNavn.keyword IS NOT NULL
| STATS total = COUNT(*),
    still_registered = COUNT(*) WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret",
    deregistered     = COUNT(*) WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Afmeldt"
  BY block_reason = KoeretoejOplysningGrundStruktur.KoeretoejBlokeringAarsagListeStruktur.KoeretoejBlokeringAarsagListe.KoeretoejBlokeringAarsag.KoeretoejBlokeringAarsagTypeNavn.keyword
| SORT total DESC | LIMIT 10

Or against the all-status flat view (equivalent, simpler):
FROM dmr-cars-all-flat
| WHERE blocking_reason == "Totalskadet køretøj"
| STATS car_count = COUNT(*) BY brand
| SORT car_count DESC | KEEP brand, car_count | LIMIT 15

Known values for blocking_reason and live counts (Personbil, all statuses, 2026-05):
  Totalskadet køretøj    561,770   (write-off / total loss / salvage)
  Skadet køretøj          21,236   (partial damage)
  Mangel                  20,143   (defect)
  Køretøjet er blokeret   12,111   (administrative block)
  Indkaldt til registrering   531
  Henvist til SKAT            172

Danish vocabulary the user might use (all route to this pattern):
  totalskadet / totalskadede / write-off / written-off / total loss / salvage → "Totalskadet køretøj"
  skadet / skadede / damaged → "Skadet køretøj"
  mangel / manglende / defect / fault → "Mangel"
  blokeret / blocked → "Køretøjet er blokeret"

PATTERN 11 — Survival rate (current vs all-time)
Use when: 'What % of [brand X] cars still exist?', 'Which brands survived best?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil" AND {optional_filter}
| STATS all_time = COUNT(*),
    current = COUNT(*) WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret"
  BY {group_field}
| WHERE all_time >= {min_count}
| EVAL survival_pct = ROUND(current * 100.0 / all_time, 1)
| SORT {sort_field} {asc_or_desc} | LIMIT 25

PATTERN 12 — Full percentile distribution
Use when: 'Show the distribution of [power/weight/CO2] for [group]?'
FROM dmr-raw-000002
| WHERE KoeretoejRegistreringStatus.keyword == "Registreret" AND KoeretoejArtNavn.keyword == "Personbil"
  AND {numeric_field} >= {min} AND {numeric_field} <= {max}
| STATS count = COUNT(*),
    p10 = PERCENTILE({numeric_field}, 10), p25 = PERCENTILE({numeric_field}, 25),
    p50 = PERCENTILE({numeric_field}, 50), p75 = PERCENTILE({numeric_field}, 75),
    p90 = PERCENTILE({numeric_field}, 90), p99 = PERCENTILE({numeric_field}, 99)
  BY {optional_group_field}
| LIMIT 15
